name: Blue Hours
prompt: Something for late night studying, melancholic and chill
tracks: [{"title":"Drunk in LA","artist":"Beach House"},{"title":"Souvlaki Space Station","artist":"Slowdive"},{"title":"Chamber of Reflection","artist":"Mac DeMarco"},{"title":"bad","artist":"wave to earth"},{"title":"The Traveller","artist":"Beach House"},{"title":"Pale Blue Eyes","artist":"The Velvet Underground"},{"title":"Here With Me","artist":"d4vd"},{"title":"West Coast","artist":"Lana Del Rey"},{"title":"When the Sun Hits","artist":"Slowdive"},{"title":"Halah","artist":"Mazzy Star"},{"title":"Cherry-Coloured Funk","artist":"Cocteau Twins"},{"title":"D>E>A>T>H>M>E>T>A>L","artist":"Panchiko"},{"title":"Chocolate and Mint","artist":"Duster"},{"title":"Paper Bag","artist":"Fiona Apple"},{"title":"I Bet on Losing Dogs","artist":"Mitski"},{"title":"Lucky","artist":"Radiohead"},{"title":"Pictures of Me","artist":"Elliott Smith"},{"title":"The Blackest Day","artist":"Lana Del Rey"},{"title":"Sleep Walk","artist":"Deftones"},{"title":"Blessing","artist":"Alex G"},{"title":"Last Night I Dreamt That Somebody Loved Me","artist":"The Smiths"},{"title":"Fade Into You","artist":"Mazzy Star"},{"title":"40 Days","artist":"Slowdive"},{"title":"Exit Music (for a Film)","artist":"Radiohead"},{"title":"Venice Bitch","artist":"Lana Del Rey"}]
thoughts: Let me dig into your listening history to find what actually fits that late-night, melancholic-chill headspace for you specifically. Good data from the track tags. Now let me fix the artist tags query and also pull your most-scrobbled artists in these vibes plus late-night listening patterns. The scrobbles normalized table is sparse — let me query raw_scrobbles and fix the alias issue. Great data. Now let me get actual track plays for the key artists that fit the vibe, and fix the late-night query. This is rich. Now I have everything I need. Let me pull a few more tracks from some of the deeper-cut artists that fit perfectly (Grouper, Ethel Cain, wave to earth, d4vd) and check their scrobble counts. I have everything I need. Let me build this now.
queries: ["SELECT tag, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM artist_tags WHERE tag IN ('chill','melancholic','ambient','lo-fi','sad','atmospheric','dreamy','introspective','slowcore','shoegaze') GROUP BY tag ORDER BY cnt DESC LIMIT 30","SELECT track, artist, tag, weight FROM track_tags WHERE tag IN ('chill','melancholic','ambient','sad','atmospheric','dreamy','introspective','slowcore','soft','quiet') ORDER BY weight DESC LIMIT 40","SELECT artist, COUNT(*) as plays FROM raw_scrobbles GROUP BY artist ORDER BY plays DESC LIMIT 40","SELECT artist, COUNT(*) as late_night_plays FROM raw_scrobbles WHERE CAST(STRFTIME('%H', scrobbled_at) AS INTEGER) >= 22 OR CAST(STRFTIME('%H', scrobbled_at) AS INTEGER) <= 4 GROUP BY artist ORDER BY late_night_plays DESC LIMIT 30"]

name: High Octane Hyperfocus
prompt: I need 90 mins of high octane music I can focus on work with. Play around with the genres.
tracks: [{"title":"Vision of Division","artist":"The Strokes"},{"title":"Territorial Pissings","artist":"Nirvana"},{"title":"Bodysnatchers","artist":"Radiohead"},{"title":"Elephant","artist":"Tame Impala"},{"title":"R U Mine?","artist":"Arctic Monkeys"},{"title":"Bull Believer","artist":"Wednesday"},{"title":"Yung Rapunxel","artist":"Azealia Banks"},{"title":"365","artist":"Charli xcx"},{"title":"Vroom Vroom","artist":"Charli xcx"},{"title":"212 (feat. Lazy Jay)","artist":"Azealia Banks"},{"title":"Kill V. Maim","artist":"Grimes"},{"title":"4ÆM","artist":"Grimes"},{"title":"Eusexua","artist":"FKA twigs"},{"title":"Ultraviolet","artist":"FKA twigs"},{"title":"Did It On'em","artist":"Nicki Minaj"},{"title":"THIQUE","artist":"Beyoncé"},{"title":"Crazy in Love (feat. JAY-Z)","artist":"Beyoncé"},{"title":"My Own Summer (Shove It)","artist":"Deftones"},{"title":"Change (In the House of Flies)","artist":"Deftones"},{"title":"Heart In a Cage","artist":"The Strokes"},{"title":"You Only Live Once","artist":"The Strokes"},{"title":"Nintendo Blood","artist":"The Voidz"},{"title":"Where No Eagles Fly","artist":"The Voidz"}]
thoughts: Let me dig into your listening data to find high-energy tracks across different genres before building this out. Good data. Now let me find high-energy tracks specifically — pulling track tags for energetic/intense moods, and digging into the heavier/more driving artists from their history. Incredible. Now I have a full picture. Let me pull a few more high-energy tracks from specific artists I want to include in the energy arc. This is everything I need. Now let me build the playlist with a real energy arc — a slow ignition that burns white-hot through the middle and exits in a blaze.
queries: ["SELECT tag, COUNT(*) as count FROM artist_tags WHERE weight > 60 GROUP BY tag ORDER BY count DESC LIMIT 40","SELECT artist_name, tag, weight FROM artist_tags WHERE tag IN ('electronic','dance','punk','hard rock','garage rock','noise rock','alternative rock','post-punk','grunge','hip-hop','hyperpop') AND weight > 70 ORDER BY weight DESC LIMIT 80","SELECT artist, track, COUNT(*) as plays FROM raw_scrobbles WHERE artist IN ('The Strokes','Arctic Monkeys','Nirvana','Deftones','Radiohead','Grimes','FKA twigs','Beyoncé','Nicki Minaj','Tame Impala','Charli xcx','Azealia Banks','Wednesday','The Voidz') GROUP BY artist, track ORDER BY plays DESC"]
